headline: Latvian Basketball Legend Uļjana Semjonova Gets Silver Screen Treatment in 'Ulya'
lede: Karlis Arnolds Avots brings the extraordinary life of Soviet basketball icon Uļjana Semjonova to vivid black-and-white life in Viesturs Kairišs' Un Certain Regard selection.
body:
In a remarkable cinematic tribute, the upcoming biographical drama 'Ulya' promises to reframe the narrative of one of basketball's most groundbreaking athletes through an intimate, unvarnished lens.

The film chronicles Semjonova's journey as a towering female athlete navigating the complex sociopolitical landscape of Soviet-era Latvia. Avots, who co-wrote the screenplay, delivers a transformative performance that explores gender, identity, and athletic ambition against a backdrop of institutional constraint.

Shooting in stark black-and-white, director Kairišs appears to emphasize the historical texture of Semjonova's remarkable trajectory. The 6'4" basketball pioneer dominated international competitions during the 1970s and 1980s, becoming a global sensation who transcended traditional gender expectations in sports.

Premièring in the prestigious Un Certain Regard section at Cannes, 'Ulya' represents a nuanced exploration of athletic excellence and personal resilience. The film signals a growing trend of sports narratives that center marginalized voices and complex historical contexts.
key_facts: Uļjana Semjonova played for Soviet national team
Avots both stars in and co-wrote the film
Movie premieres at 2026 Cannes Film Festival
